---
title: "Examples"
engine: python
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{python}
import shinylive

def shinylive_example_url(name: str) -> str:
    return shinylive.url_encode(
        f"examples/{name}/app.py",
        f"examples/{name}/requirements.txt",
    )
```

## Interactive TreeView Examples

This page demonstrates the `shiny-treeview` component through interactive examples. Each example showcases different features and use cases, and you can edit and run the code directly in your browser.

## File Browser

A classic file browser interface with single selection. Click on files and folders to see how selection works, and notice how different file types show different action buttons.

::: {.content-visible when-format="markdown"}
[Demo **file browser** app on shinylive.io](`{python} shinylive_example_url("file-browser")`).
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
::: column-page-inset
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: horizontal
#| viewerHeight: 500

## file: app.py
{{< include "examples/file-browser/app.py" >}}

## file: requirements.txt
{{< include "examples/file-browser/requirements.txt" >}}
```
:::
:::

## Multiple Selection

This example shows a task management interface where you can select multiple items at once. Great for batch operations like marking multiple tasks as complete or organizing project work.

::: {.content-visible when-format="markdown"}
[Demo **multiple selection** app on shinylive.io](`{python} shinylive_example_url("multiple-selection")`).
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
::: column-page-inset
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: horizontal
#| viewerHeight: 500

## file: app.py
{{< include "examples/multiple-selection/app.py" >}}

## file: requirements.txt
{{< include "examples/multiple-selection/requirements.txt" >}}
```
:::
:::

## Checkbox Selection

This example demonstrates checkbox-style selection, perfect for component selection interfaces. Users can clearly see what's selected with checkboxes, making it ideal for configuration or setup screens.

::: {.content-visible when-format="markdown"}
[Demo **checkbox selection** app on shinylive.io](`{python} shinylive_example_url("checkbox-selection")`).
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
::: column-page-inset
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: horizontal
#| viewerHeight: 500

## file: app.py
{{< include "examples/checkbox-selection/app.py" >}}

## file: requirements.txt
{{< include "examples/checkbox-selection/requirements.txt" >}}
```
:::
:::

## Key Features Demonstrated

- **Single Selection**: Choose one item at a time (file browser example)
- **Multiple Selection**: Select multiple items with Ctrl/Cmd+click (task management)
- **Checkbox Selection**: Visual checkboxes for clear selection state (component picker)
- **Pre-selected Items**: Set initial selections with the `selected` parameter
- **Expandable Nodes**: Control which folders are expanded initially with the `expanded` parameter
- **Disabled Items**: Mark certain items as non-selectable with the `disabled` parameter
- **Hierarchical Data**: Support for deeply nested tree structures using `TreeItem` objects
- **Dynamic UI**: Update other UI elements based on tree selection

## Usage Patterns

### File Management
- File browsers and explorers
- Document organization systems
- Asset management interfaces
- Directory navigation

### Data Organization
- Database schema exploration
- API endpoint browsing
- Category and tag selection
- Hierarchical data visualization

### Configuration
- Feature selection interfaces
- Component configuration screens
- Settings organization
- Multi-step wizards

## Getting Started

To use `shiny-treeview` in your own project:

```bash
pip install shiny-treeview
```

Then import and use the component:

```python
from shiny_treeview import input_treeview, TreeItem
```

## Running Examples Locally

You can find all these examples in the [docs/examples](https://github.com/davidchall/shiny-treeview/tree/main/docs/examples) directory. To run them locally:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/davidchall/shiny-treeview.git
cd shiny-treeview/docs/examples/file-browser
pip install -r requirements.txt
python -m shiny run app.py
```

> **Note**: When using shiny-treeview on [shinylive.io](https://shinylive.io), remember to add `shiny-treeview` to your `requirements.txt` file!

Check out the [Reference](reference/) for detailed API documentation.
